Let $y = f(x)$ and $y = g(x)$ be two continuous positive functions intersecting only at three points $(0,1)$, $(3,4)$ and $(5,6)$. Define $h(x) = \max\{f(x), g(x)\}$ as $h(x) = \begin{cases} f(x), & 0 \le x < 3 \\ g(x), & 3 \le x \le 5 \end{cases}$. If $\int_0^5 f(x) \, dx = a$, $\int_0^5 g(x) \, dx = b$, $\int_3^5 f(x) \, dx = c$, $\int_0^3 g(x) \, dx = d$, then the area of region bounded between $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ from $x = 0$ to $x = 5$ is
qOptions: $\int_0^3 f(x) \, dx + \int_3^5 f(x) \, dx + \int_0^3 g(x) \, dx + \int_3^5 g(x) \, dx$
